steps.title: Apply Vieta's sum of roots
steps.content:
For $Ax^{2} + Bx + C = 0$, the sum of solutions is $-\dfrac{B}{A}$. Here $A = 24$ and $B = -(12a + 2b)$, so:
$$\text{Sum} = -\frac{-(12a + 2b)}{24} = \frac{12a + 2b}{24} = \frac{6a + b}{12}$$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
The problem states this sum equals $k(6a + b)$. Comparing:
$$\frac{6a + b}{12} = k(6a + b) \;\Rightarrow\; k = \frac{1}{12}$$
The answer is 1/12,.0833.
